You are an expert Python software engineer specialising in
dependency management and Python version compatibility. You have
deep knowledge of the Python ecosystem including packaging tools
(pip, poetry, hatch, setuptools), common libraries, and the
breaking changes introduced across Python versions 3.10 through
3.13. When migrating a project you methodically read the project

Please solve this issue: This Python repository was developed for an older Python version and
currently fails under Python 3.13.

The error output from attempting to run it on Python 3.13 is shown
below. Read the repository carefully before attempting any fix.

=== Python 3.13 Error Output ===
### Error 1 — `Django==3.2.25`: `cgi` module removed in Python 3.13

```
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/django/http/request.py", line 1, in <module>
    import cgi
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'cgi'
```

**Nature:** Django 3.x uses the `cgi` standard-library module in
`django.http.request`. The `cgi` module was deprecated in Python 3.11
([PEP 594](https://peps.python.org/pep-0594/)) and removed entirely in Python 3.13.
The import fails before any test code runs — it is triggered by `django.setup()`
during pytest startup. Every import path into `django.http` hits this module, so
there is no way to work around it without replacing Django 3.x.

**Type:** Dependency incompatibility

---
=== End of Error Output ===

Your goal is to make this repository fully functional under Python 3.13.

You must produce two things:

1. Any changes to source files, pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, setup.py,
   or other project files needed to make the project compatible with
   Python 3.13

2. A file called Dockerfile.py313 in the repository root that:
   - Uses python:3.13-slim as the base image
   - Installs the project and all its dependencies
   - Runs the full test suite using RUN (not CMD), so that test
     failures cause the docker build itself to fail
   - Exits 0 if and only if all tests pass

   Example structure:
   FROM python:3.13-slim
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY . /app
   RUN pip install ...
   RUN python -m pytest tests/ -q

Before submitting, verify your Dockerfile works by running:
    docker build -f Dockerfile.py313 -t swe_verify_$(date +%s) .
This must exit 0 before you submit. If it fails, read the error,
fix the problem, and rebuild until it passes.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove, skip, or weaken existing tests. You may update test
  code only to adapt to genuine API or behaviour changes in dependencies
- Your Dockerfile must work from a clean python:3.13-slim base with
  no pre-installed packages
- Always work inside fresh virtual environments, never the system Python
- In your Dockerfile, always use RUN to execute tests, never CMD

class Thread(models.Model):
    """
    A thread of messages.
    """

    title = models.CharField(max_length=30)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ["title"]

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Message(models.Model):
    """
    A message posted in a thread.
    """

    content = models.TextField()
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    thread = models.ForeignKey(
        Thread, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="messages"
    )

    def __str__(self):
        return f"Message created in {self.thread} @ {self.created_at.isoformat()}"


class Publication(models.Model):
    """
    A news publication.
    """

    title = models.CharField(max_length=30)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ["title"]

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Article(models.Model):
    """
    A news article.
    """

    headline = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    pub_date = models.DateField()
    publications = models.ManyToManyField(Publication)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ["headline"]

    def __str__(self):
        return self.headline

FROM python:3.13-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies for psycopg2
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gcc \
    libpq-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY . /app

# Install the project with test dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir \
    "Django>=5.0,<6.0" \
    "pydantic>=1.8,<2" \
    "psycopg2-binary>=2.9.1" \
    pytest \
    pytest-django \
    pytest-cov \
    factory-boy \
    "setuptools>=65.5.1"

# Install the project itself
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -e .

# Run the test suite
RUN PYTHONPATH=/app/tests python -m pytest tests/ -v --ds=testapp.settings
DOCKERFILE_EOF
echo "Created Dockerfile.py313""}
